title: Cryogenic refrigerator, cryogenic refrigerator system and method for operating a cryogenic refrigerator
abstract: A cryogenic refrigerator (10) of Gifford-McMahon (GM) type comprising a refrigeration volume (12); a reciprocating displacer (14) movable within the refrigeration volume (12); a refrigeration valve (16) to supply and exhaust of refrigeration fluid to and from the refrigeration volume (12); and a pneumatic drive device (18) operated with pneumatic fluid to move the displacer and/or for operating the refrigeration valve (16). Further, a cryogenic refrigerator system and a method for operating a cryogenic refrigerator of Gifford-McMahon (GM) type.
claims: Claims 1. A cryogenic refrigerator (10) of Gifford-McMahon (GM) type comprising: a refrigeration volume (12); a reciprocating displacer (14) movable within the refrigeration volume (12); a refrigeration valve (16) to supply and exhaust of refrigeration fluid to and from the refrigeration volume; and a pneumatic drive device (18) operated with pneumatic fluid to move the displacer (14) and/or for operating the refrigeration valve (16). 2. Cryogenic refrigerator (10) according to claim 1, wherein the pneumatic drive device (18) is a, preferably rotational, pneumatic motor. 3. Cryogenic refrigerator (10) according to claim 2, further comprising a device for transforming rotational motion of the pneumatic motor to translational motion. 4. Cryogenic refrigerator (10) according to any one of claims 1-3, further comprising a housing (11), wherein the housing (11) has a high-pressure port (64) connected to the pneumatic drive (18) and to the refrigeration valve (16) to supply high-pressure fluid, and/or wherein the housing has a low-pressure port (68) connected to the pneumatic drive (18) and to the refrigeration valve (16) to exhaust low-pressure fluid. 5. Cryogenic refrigerator (10) according to any one of claims 1-4, further comprising a compressor (32), preferably a single compressor, connected to the refrigeration valve (16) and to the pneumatic drive device (18). 6. Cryogenic refrigerator (10) according to any one of claims 1-5, wherein the pneumatic fluid and refrigeration fluid is the same fluid or a different fluid. 7. Cryogenic refrigerator (10) according to any one of claims 1-6, further comprising a pneumatic drive valve, particularly a throttle valve, to control the pneumatic fluid supplied to and/or exhausted from the pneumatic drive device (18). 8. Cryogenic refrigerator (10) according to claim 7, wherein the pneumatic drive valve is adapted to control operation of the pneumatic drive device (18), particularly the driving speed, of the pneumatic drive device (18). 9. Cryogenic refrigerator (10) according to claim 7 or 8, further comprising a pneumatic drive sensor (31) to measure operation of the pneumatic drive device (18), particularly the driving speed of the pneumatic drive device (18). 10. Cryogenic refrigerator (10) according to claim 9, further comprising a controller to control the pneumatic drive valve based on a value measured by the pneumatic drive sensor (31). 11. A cryogenic refrigerator system, comprising: - a cryogenic refrigerator (10) according to any one of claims 1-10; and - an electro-magnetic sensitive device cooled by the cryogenic refrigerator (10). 12. Cryogenic refrigerator system according to claim 11, wherein the electromagnetic sensitive device is a medical device, particularly a medical imaging device; or a quantum computer; or a radio telescope. 13. Method for operating a cryogenic refrigerator (10) of Gifford-McMahon (GM) type, wherein a reciprocating displacer and/or a refrigeration valve (16) is operated pneumatically by means of a pneumatic drive device (18). 14. Method according to claim 13, wherein a refrigeration volume of the cryogenic refrigerator (10) as well as the reciprocating displacer and/or the refrigeration valve (16) are operated by: the same compressor (32) and/or the same fluid and/or the same fluid cycle and/or the same high pressure fluid line and/or the same low pressure fluid line. 15. Method according to claim 13 or 14, wherein the method is carried out with a cryogenic refrigerator (10) according to any one of claims 1-10 or a cryogenic refrigerator system according to claim 11 or 12.
